title: Air Quality & VOC Monitoring
dataSources: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `sourcetype=edge_hub` — built-in VOC sensor (optional, <1s response, IAQ score)
app: Splunk Edge Hub (optional air quality sensor), Splunk OT Intelligence
spl:
| mstats avg(_value) as value
  where index=edge-hub-data AND metric_name IN (voc, humidity, temperature)
  span=15m by metric_name, extracted_host
description: Indoor air quality affects occupant health and productivity. Edge Hub's optional VOC sensor provides IAQ scoring for workplace wellness monitoring.
value: Indoor air quality affects occupant health and productivity. Edge Hub's optional VOC sensor provides IAQ scoring for workplace wellness monitoring.
implementation: Deploy Edge Hub with optional VOC/air quality sensor module. The sensor provides IAQ (Indoor Air Quality) score with <1 second response time. Data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data` index. Note: Edge Hub measures VOC and IAQ score — it does not have a CO2 or PM2.5 sensor natively. For CO2/PM2.5, connect external sensors via MQTT or I²C. Alert when IAQ score exceeds thresholds. Correlate with humidity sensor data for comfort indexing.
visualization: Line chart (IAQ score over time), Gauge (current IAQ), Multi-metric dashboard (VOC + humidity + temperature).
knownFalsePositives: AQI spikes during cooking, cleaning, paint or solvent use, or HVAC maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (optional air quality sensor), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `sourcetype=edge_hub` — built-in VOC sensor (optional, <1s response, IAQ score).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy Edge Hub with optional VOC/air quality sensor module. The sensor provides IAQ (Indoor Air Quality) score with <1 second response time. Data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data` index. Note: Edge Hub measures VOC and IAQ score — it does not have a CO2 or PM2.5 sensor natively. For CO2/PM2.5, connect external sensors via MQTT or I²C. Alert when IAQ score exceeds thresholds. Correlate with humidity sensor data for comfort indexing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(_value) as value
  where index=edge-hub-data AND metric_name IN (voc, humidity, temperature)
  span=15m by metric_name, extracted_host
```

Understanding this SPL

**Air Quality & VOC Monitoring** — Indoor air quality affects occupant health and productivity. Edge Hub's optional VOC sensor provides IAQ scoring for workplace wellness monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `sourcetype=edge_hub` — built-in VOC sensor (optional, <1s response, IAQ score). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (optional air quality sensor), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-data.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (IAQ score over time), Gauge (current IAQ), Multi-metric dashboard (VOC + humidity + temperature).
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with fumes, dust, and other air quality issues near people before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for CIM; ensure metrics index access and mstats permissions for the indexes in the search.
